questions.questionText: The most common type of breast cancer is:
questions.optionB: Invasive ductal carcinoma (IDC — accounts for 70-80% of all breast cancers)
questions.explanation: Invasive ductal carcinoma (IDC/NST — no special type): 70-80% of breast cancers. Presents as hard, irregular mass with spiculated margins on mammography. Invasive lobular carcinoma (ILC): 10-15%, bilateral risk, signet ring cells, E-cadherin negative. DCIS: pre-invasive, microcalcifications on mammography. Paget's disease: eczematous nipple changes (underlying DCIS/invasive cancer). Inflammatory breast cancer: peau d'orange, worst prognosis. Reference: Bailey & Love — Breast Cancer.

questions.questionText: Triple assessment for breast lump includes:
questions.optionB: Clinical examination + imaging (mammography/USG) + tissue diagnosis (FNAC/core biopsy)
questions.explanation: Triple assessment: gold standard for evaluating breast lumps. (1) Clinical examination (inspection + palpation). (2) Imaging: mammography (>35 years — microcalcifications, masses), ultrasonography (<35 years — distinguished solid vs cystic). (3) Tissue diagnosis: FNAC (C1-C5 cytology) or core needle biopsy (histology — preferred, provides architecture). All three must be concordant. If discordant → excision biopsy. Reference: Bailey & Love — Triple Assessment.

questions.questionText: Modified radical mastectomy (MRM) involves removal of:
questions.optionB: Entire breast + axillary lymph nodes (levels I, II) — pectoralis major muscle is PRESERVED
questions.explanation: MRM (Patey/Auchincloss): removal of entire breast + overlying skin + nipple-areola complex + axillary lymph nodes (level I and II). Pectoralis major: PRESERVED (unlike radical/Halsted mastectomy which removes both pectoralis muscles). Most common surgery for breast cancer in India. Sentinel lymph node biopsy (SLNB): alternative to axillary dissection in clinically node-negative patients (dye + technetium mapping). BCS (breast-conserving surgery/lumpectomy) + radiation: equivalent survival to mastectomy for early-stage. Reference: Bailey & Love — MRM.

questions.questionText: Fibroadenoma of the breast is characterized by:
questions.optionB: Well-defined, mobile, painless, rubbery mass ('breast mouse') — most common benign breast tumor in young women
questions.explanation: Fibroadenoma: most common benign breast tumor in women 15-35 years. Features: well-circumscribed, firm, rubbery, highly mobile ('breast mouse'), painless, non-tender. USG: well-defined, homogeneous, hypoechoic. Grows under estrogen influence (may enlarge during pregnancy). Usually single, can be multiple. Management: <3cm in young → observe/reassure. Excision if: enlarging, >3cm, patient anxiety. Giant fibroadenoma: >5cm. Phyllodes tumor: similar presentation but rapid growth, may be malignant. Reference: Bailey & Love — Fibroadenoma.
